# hysteron

## NAME

**hysteron**

## SYNOPSIS

`hysteron [OPTIONS]`

## OPTIONS

### Help Options

* `-h`, `--help` -
  Show this help message
* `-v`, `--version` -
  Show version information

### Logging

* `--log-level` -
  log verbosity (trace is verbose; use for short-lived diagnostics)
  * Defaults: `info`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_LOG_LEVEL`
  * Choices: `trace, debug, info, warn, error`
* `--log-format` -
  log output format (text or JSON)
  * Defaults: `text`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_LOG_FORMAT`
  * Choices: `text, json`
* `--log-output` -
  log destination: stdout, stderr, or a file path
  * Defaults: `stderr`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_LOG_OUTPUT`
* `--log-file-mode` -
  when output is a file: append or truncate existing content
  * Defaults: `append`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_LOG_FILE_MODE`
  * Choices: `append, truncate`
* `--log-time-format` -
  timestamp: Go layout or rfc3339|rfc3339nano|unix|unixms|unixmicro|unixnano
  * Defaults: `2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z07:00`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_LOG_TIME_FORMAT`
* `--log-color-policy` -
  console colors: auto honors NO_COLOR, FORCE_COLOR, and TTY detection
  * Defaults: `auto`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_LOG_COLOR_POLICY`
  * Choices: `auto, always, never`

## COMMANDS

**Help Commands**

### help

Show help

**Usage:** `hysteron [OPTIONS] help`

#### Help Options

* `-h`, `--help` -
  Show this help message
* `-v`, `--version` -
  Show version information

### version

Show version information

**Usage:** `hysteron [OPTIONS] version [version-OPTIONS]`

#### Show version information

* `--short` -
  Print version number only
* `--commit` -
  Print commit SHA only
* `--json` -
  Print version information as JSON

#### Help Options

* `-h`, `--help` -
  Show this help message
* `-v`, `--version` -
  Show version information

### completion

Generate shell completion

**Usage:** `hysteron [OPTIONS] completion [completion-OPTIONS]`

#### Generate shell completion

* `--shell` -
  Shell completion format
  * Choices: `bash, zsh, pwsh`

#### Help Options

* `-h`, `--help` -
  Show this help message
* `-v`, `--version` -
  Show version information

#### Arguments

* `output`
  * Description: Output file path

**Management Commands**

### cluster

Manage cluster control operations

**Usage:** `hysteron [OPTIONS] cluster [cluster-OPTIONS]`

#### Common

* `-c`, `--cluster-name` -
  cluster name
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_CLUSTER_NAME`

#### Kubernetes

* `--k8s-config` -
  path to kubeconfig file. Overrides $KUBECONFIG
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_K8S_CONFIG`
* `--k8s-resource-kind` -
  k8s resource kind used to store cluster data
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_K8S_RESOURCE_KIND`
  * Choices: `configmap, secret`
* `--k8s-resource-name` -
  k8s resource name template; {cluster} is replaced with the cluster name
  * Defaults: `hysteron-{cluster}`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_K8S_RESOURCE_NAME`
* `--k8s-context` -
  kubeconfig context name
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_K8S_CONTEXT`
* `--k8s-namespace` -
  kubernetes namespace name
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_K8S_NAMESPACE`

#### Store

* `--store-backend` -
  store backend type
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_BACKEND`
  * Choices: `etcd, etcdv3, kubernetes, k8s`
* `--store-endpoints` -
  comma-separated list of store endpoints
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_ENDPOINTS`
* `--store-prefix` -
  store base prefix
  * Defaults: `hysteron/cluster`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_PREFIX`
* `--store-cert-file` -
  certificate file for store client identification
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_CERT_FILE`
* `--store-key` -
  private key file for store client identification
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_KEY`
* `--store-ca-file` -
  CA bundle for HTTPS-enabled store servers
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_CA_FILE`
* `--store-timeout` -
  store request timeout
  * Defaults: `5s`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_TIMEOUT`
* `--store-skip-tls-verify` -
  skip store certificate verification (insecure)
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_SKIP_TLS_VERIFY`

#### Help Options

* `-h`, `--help` -
  Show this help message
* `-v`, `--version` -
  Show version information

  Show version information

  Show this help message
* `-v`, `--version` -
  Show version information

### cluster data write

Replace current cluster data

**Usage:** `hysteron [OPTIONS] cluster [cluster-OPTIONS] data write [write-OPTIONS]`

#### Replace current cluster data

* `-f`, `--file` -
  file containing the replacement cluster data
* `-y`, `--yes` -
  do not ask for confirmation

* `-h`, `--help` -
  Show this help message
* `-v`, `--version` -
  Show version information

### cluster update

Replace or patch the current cluster specification

**Usage:** `hysteron [OPTIONS] cluster [cluster-OPTIONS] update [update-OPTIONS]`

#### Replace or patch the current cluster specification

* `-f`, `--file` -
  file containing a complete cluster specification or a patch to apply to the
  current cluster specification
* `-p`, `--patch` -
  patch the current cluster specification instead of replacing it

#### Help Options

* `-h`, `--help` -
  Show this help message
* `-v`, `--version` -
  Show version information

### failover

Manage failover operations

**Usage:** `hysteron [OPTIONS] failover [failover-OPTIONS]`

#### Common

* `-c`, `--cluster-name` -
  cluster name
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_CLUSTER_NAME`

#### Kubernetes

* `--k8s-config` -
  path to kubeconfig file. Overrides $KUBECONFIG
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_K8S_CONFIG`
* `--k8s-resource-kind` -
  k8s resource kind used to store cluster data
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_K8S_RESOURCE_KIND`
  * Choices: `configmap, secret`
* `--k8s-resource-name` -
  k8s resource name template; {cluster} is replaced with the cluster name
  * Defaults: `hysteron-{cluster}`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_K8S_RESOURCE_NAME`

  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_K8S_NAMESPACE`

#### Store

* `--store-backend` -
  store backend type
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_BACKEND`
  * Choices: `etcd, etcdv3, kubernetes, k8s`
* `--store-endpoints` -
  comma-separated list of store endpoints
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_ENDPOINTS`
* `--store-prefix` -
  store base prefix
  * Defaults: `hysteron/cluster`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_PREFIX`
* `--store-cert-file` -
  certificate file for store client identification
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_CERT_FILE`
* `--store-key` -
  private key file for store client identification
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_KEY`
* `--store-ca-file` -
  CA bundle for HTTPS-enabled store servers
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_CA_FILE`
* `--store-timeout` -
  store request timeout
  * Defaults: `5s`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_TIMEOUT`
* `--store-skip-tls-verify` -
  skip store certificate verification (insecure)
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_STORE_SKIP_TLS_VERIFY`

  Show this help message
* `-v`, `--version` -
  Show version information

### keeper

Run keeper runtime components

**Usage:** `hysteron [OPTIONS] keeper [keeper-OPTIONS]`

#### Common

* `-c`, `--cluster-name` -
  cluster name
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_CLUSTER_NAME`

#### Metrics

* `--metrics-listen-address` -
  metrics listen address i.e "0.0.0.0:8080" (disabled by default)
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_METRICS_LISTEN_ADDRESS`

* `-h`, `--help` -
  Show this help message
* `-v`, `--version` -
  Show version information

  store request timeout

  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_ETCD_TIMEOUT`

  skip store certificate verification (insecure)
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_ETCD_SKIP_TLS_VERIFY`

  Show this help message

### proxy

Run proxy runtime components

**Usage:** `hysteron [OPTIONS] proxy [proxy-OPTIONS]`

#### Common

* `-c`, `--cluster-name` -
  cluster name
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_CLUSTER_NAME`

#### Metrics

* `--metrics-listen-address` -
  metrics listen address i.e "0.0.0.0:8080" (disabled by default)
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_METRICS_LISTEN_ADDRESS`

#### Proxy

* `--listen-address` -
  proxy listening address
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_LISTEN_ADDRESS`
* `--port` -
  proxy listening port
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_PORT`
* `--disable-writable-listener` -
  disable the writable proxy listener
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_DISABLE_WRITABLE_LISTENER`
* `--read-only-listen-address` -
  read-only proxy listening address
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_READ_ONLY_LISTEN_ADDRESS`
* `--read-only-port` -
  read-only proxy listening port
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_READ_ONLY_PORT`

#### Help Options

* `-h`, `--help` -
  Show this help message
* `-v`, `--version` -
  Show version information

### sentinel

Run sentinel runtime components

**Usage:** `hysteron [OPTIONS] sentinel [sentinel-OPTIONS]`

#### Common

* `-c`, `--cluster-name` -
  cluster name
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_CLUSTER_NAME`

#### Metrics

* `--metrics-listen-address` -
  metrics listen address i.e "0.0.0.0:8080" (disabled by default)
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_METRICS_LISTEN_ADDRESS`

#### Sentinel

* `-f`, `--initial-cluster-spec` -
  a file providing the initial cluster specification, used only at cluster
  initialization, ignored if cluster is already initialized
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_INITIAL_CLUSTER_SPEC`
* `--cluster-spec` -
  per-cluster initial cluster specification override as <cluster-name>=<path>;
  can be repeated
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_CLUSTER_SPEC`

#### Web

* `--web-listen-address` -
  web status dashboard listen address, for example 0.0.0.0:8081 (disabled by
  default)
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_WEB_LISTEN_ADDRESS`
* `--web-base-path` -
  base path prefix for web UI and API routes
  * Defaults: `/`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_WEB_BASE_PATH`
* `--web-auth-username` -
  optional HTTP Basic auth username for web endpoints
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_WEB_AUTH_USERNAME`
* `--web-auth-password` -
  optional HTTP Basic auth password for web endpoints
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_WEB_AUTH_PASSWORD`
* `--web-read-timeout` -
  maximum duration for reading the entire request, including the body
  * Defaults: `5s`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_WEB_READ_TIMEOUT`
* `--web-write-timeout` -
  maximum duration before timing out writes of the response
  * Defaults: `10s`
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_WEB_WRITE_TIMEOUT`
* `--web-allow-unsafe-admin-without-auth` -
  allow admin API endpoints when web auth is disabled (unsafe; intended only for
  controlled environments)
  * Environment: `$HYSTERON_WEB_ALLOW_UNSAFE_ADMIN_WITHOUT_AUTH`

#### Help Options

* `-h`, `--help` -
  Show this help message
* `-v`, `--version` -
  Show version information
